name: jamf-pro
description: Reads Jamf Pro buildings, departments, categories, and scripts through the Jamf Pro REST API using Basic-credential token-exchange authentication.

risk.read: external Jamf Pro API read of MDM configuration data (buildings, departments, categories, scripts)
risk.approval: none; read-only, no reverse-ETL write surface

conformance.reason: custom-auth-only (mode:custom AuthHook, no when-gated non-custom fallback); the AuthHook's real token-exchange target is config.base_url + /v1/auth/token -- conformance's synthetic non-secret config sets base_url to the literal string "synthetic-conformance-value", never the replay server's own address (withReplayURL only overrides the bundle's HTTP.URL field used for stream/check request resolution, not RuntimeConfig.Config["base_url"] the hook itself reads), so the hook's real HTTP POST can never land on the replay server; every auth-resolving dynamic check would otherwise fail identically and uninformatively trying to dial an invalid host. Hook-covered, proven by internal/connectors/hooks/jamf-pro/hooks_test.go (TestAuthenticator_ExchangesBasicForBearerAndCaches: Basic-credential token exchange against a real httptest server, Bearer-token reuse/caching across calls, and TestAuthenticator_RequiresBaseURLUsernamePassword's required-field coverage) plus the declarative pagination/records/schema shape below, which every OTHER goldens/pilot bundle's own conformance run already proves generically for page_number+start_page:0 streams (see hubplanner's identical pagination shape in the same wave).
